meaning: adj.鼻子大的,好管闲事的,爱追问的n.鼻子很大的人,好管闲事的人
examples: He was having to whisper in order to avoid being overheard by their nosy neighbours.
The people in the hall seemed very nosy, keeping their eyes on me with curiosity.
Don't be so nosy—it's none of your business.
So I did what any enterprising (read: nosy) reporter would do: I reported around them.
TechCrunch optimistically assumes this means Facebook is going to start slapping nosy employers with lawsuits.
